title: CANN TBE dependency pre-flight: install decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, protobuf before any CANN operator execution on Ascend NPU
problem_description: CANN 8.5.0 TBE (Tensor Boost Engine) / TE (Tik Engine) packages import decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, and protobuf at runtime. When these packages are absent from the virtual environment, CANN TBE initialization fails with cryptic ImportError or AttributeError messages that do not clearly indicate which package is missing. Each missing dependency requires a separate pip install + retry cycle, adding 5+ unnecessary repair iterations to Phase 5 validation. This affects ALL projects that use any CANN custom operator (aclnn, aclop, tbe), not just custom-op projects.
rough_fix_approach: Add a pre-flight dependency installation step in Phase 2 (venv creation) or early Phase 5 (validation setup) that installs all known CANN TBE transitive dependencies before any operator execution: `pip install decorator attrs psutil cffi protobuf`. A companion pre-flight checker can verify importability: `.venv/bin/python -c "modules=['decorator','attrs','scipy','psutil','cffi','protobuf']; [__import__(m) or True for m in modules]"`. This eliminates the iterative repair cycle entirely.
artifact_evidence: validated/phase_5_validation_canonical.json (noted in notes field: CANN TBE dependency resolution)
validated/phase_2_venv_create_canonical.json (initial venv creation did not include these dependencies)
output_projects/04_Deepwave_20260602_213331/.venv/ (site-packages: decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, protobuf installed after repair)

involved_code_files.role: CANN TBE/TE runtime — imports decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, protobuf at import time

reasoning: This is a hard dependency constraint that affects EVERY project migrated to CANN 8.x on Ascend NPU. The missing packages cause cryptic errors that waste multiple Phase 5 repair iterations. The fix is fully deterministic — either the packages are installed and TBE imports work, or they're not and it fails. No project code changes are needed. This can be integrated into Phase 2 venv creation as a standard post-install step, making it a reusable skill. Confidence is 0.95 because the dependency list is static for a given CANN version, the error signature is reproducible, and the fix is trivially verifiable.
